task: Add the next Pro Rust product-core slice: a server-side workspace access gate that evaluates a requested Pro action against the local preview workspace/actor context, exposes a keyless API endpoint for gate decisions, renders the decision in the graph-first web shell, and synchronizes Pro docs/evidence. Keep this local-only and keyless: do not add sessions, passwords, external auth providers, JWT validation, credential storage, provider execution, quota/billing mutation, worker execution, OSS runtime changes, npm dependencies, package publishing, or live provider calls.
securityRequirements: Mention auth, secrets, permissions, and sensitive-data handling explicitly.
Do not accept or store passwords, tokens, provider secrets, or API keys.
dependencyRequirements: Mention new or upgraded packages, lockfile changes, and dependency impact explicitly.
performanceRequirements: Mention performance, latency, or load impact in evidence.
understandingRequirements: Explain the main tradeoffs so future maintainers can follow the change.
continuityRequirements: Mention reuse targets and any deliberate continuity break in evidence.
patternSummary: Reuse the existing domain payload builders, Axum state/endpoint style, web fetchJson helper, and compact graph-first panels.
smallestViableChange: Add a deterministic local workspace access gate payload and surface it through one API endpoint plus one web panel.
assumptions: The active Pro slice remains local preview-only.
The OSS runtime remains keyless and untouched.
acceptanceCriteria: Domain tests prove preview/read actions can pass while live/provider/worker/write actions remain denied.
API tests prove the endpoint returns server-computed decisions and no secret-shaped fields.
Web tests prove the graph-first shell contains the new gate panel, endpoint path, and renderer.
Docs and evidence explain that this is not real hosted auth.
expectedBehaviorChanges: Pro API exposes a keyless workspace access gate decision endpoint.
Pro web shell renders whether a requested action is allowed, preview-only, or denied by hosted blockers.
userVisibleEffects: The Pro graph-first shell can show the first server-side access decision boundary before live execution exists.
riskJustification: This is a keyless local gate evaluator that denies live/provider/worker/write actions. It prepares a real server-side decision boundary without enabling auth sessions, credentials, provider execution, or billing.

session.taskRequest: Continue Pro by adding a server-side workspace access gate before real provider/search execution.

finishCheckHints: Report the commands you actually ran: cargo test --manifest-path pro/Cargo.toml
Finish with agent-guardrails check --review before handing off or merging.
archaeologyNotes: The automated guardrails plan inferred build artifacts and OSS paths; the contract was manually narrowed to the Rust Pro workspace paths actually used by this slice.
nextActions: Inspect the existing domain workspace access payload and API/web patterns.
Implement the smallest server-side gate evaluator.
Run required commands and update evidence.
